mentions.note: Market leader with strong WWDC announcements (AI integration with Google/Gemini making Siri smarter). Traders sold it wrongly. Apple will keep improving and is critical to market leadership. Deserves to keep winning.

Caller Hope asked long-term entry. Trading at 47x earnings, $974. Cramer: buy some now, hope pullback to 45x. Worst case runs to $1,025, then exit. Buy value incrementally rather than all-in at one level.

Botched IPO; opened at $370 vs $170-180 expected. Caller Angel and hundreds of thousands underwater on secondary purchases. Misallocation by underwriters. Cut position in half; if rallies more, get rid of rest. Cautionary tale for SpaceX risk.
Caller Dave asked about post-earnings position. Avoid entire cosmetics group despite Elf being good company. Sector milieu is troubled; can't buck a troubled industry even with solid fundamentals.

Caller Adam held since 2001, asking about buying more. Cramer: 3% yield is magical level for HD. Buy more at 20x earnings. Fed won't cut rates—higher discount rates make dividend yield more valuable in current environment.

Data center power generation; IPO'd last Thursday at $27, now $33.72. Equipment orders +60% YoY (Q1), backlog 7x growth to $4.78B. EBITDA margin 20.8% (second best in peer group). Enterprise multiple 46x justified by growth and AI tailwinds. Prefers pullback to $29 (GE Vernova parity) but small position acceptable now.
Expensive SaaS stock with ongoing pivot. Better than most enterprise software peers but not time to buy in cautious environment. Cramer has mixed feelings.

Off-price retail leader up 47% under CEO Jim Conroy. 17% comp growth, 20% revenue growth, 27% earnings growth despite consumer pressure. Better merchandising, marketing, in-store experience driving outperformance. Runway to 3,600 stores from 2,300. Absolutely worth owning; off-price one of few working retail segments.

Bullish long-term for patient, multi-generational investors (projects may take lifetimes). IPO Friday oversubscribed with risk of opening too high then crashing hard (Cerebrus scenario). Avoid market orders at opening; wait for post-IPO weakness. Near-term IPO mechanics fraught despite solid fundamentals.
